content:
YOU WERE WARNED, if you're coming in without having read a2ch5 expect heavy spoilers bc i'm diving into EVERYTHING about arachne and nefraea from my perspective and taking it all apart, from pv to radio drama to story at some point because they make me ill and sick and my brain runs in silly little circles on its little spinning wheel they're keeping me trapped on

so!! starting with nefraea's whole existence, because really, who is she? arachne created her to be "free" when she was forced to cut off/end her birth world, because she wanted a part of herself that wasn't touched/tainted by the forces of having to obey "fate". she says goodbye to her old self/prior existence as she does it, thanking the "spring" that is nefraea as she does her job and leaves it all behind her
and so that ends up with the radio drama, where arachne "kills" nefraea after some time, banishing her to the abyssal prison, and so nefraea grows to "despise" arachne, not truly hating her, but rather what she represents, that being the "fate" she has to obey. we don't really know why arachne made the decision to then cut her away after so long, but i expect that to be revealed eventually (if it isn't biav is going to have to throw hands with me)
so when nefraea is freed from the abyssal prison, since she came from the initial desire to be free, her goal becomes to "free" arachne from "fate", by taking over the role, and since unlike arachne, who is bound by herself in a way (rea/leng spider maid), she does what she wants, causing the "chaos" in arachne's theater and tainting the threads of different worlds to get her attention, and then "frees" her by putting arachne under her control (5-10)
thread_title: Arachne story rambles (arc2 ch5 spoilers for basically the ENTIRE THING)

content:
there's also the significance of "memories" and the others, who i'm personally taking as the three big driving factors for arachne, with "kindness", "romance" and "memories" all being things that she can never have nor give, since she chose to separate herself from them in order to not be as hurt by her own actions in severing threads of fate. i'm choosing to interpret their names also quite literally, since arachne can never give "kindness" to the worlds she knows will end, she can never have "romance" when she always knows how it'll end, and "memories" only tie her down, so she cuts them out and makes them separate entities from herself

additionally, with nefraea begging arachne to never leave her in the end of 5-11, it's also showed that the events of the radio drama (arachne "betraying" nefraea and banishing her) impacted nefraea so heavily that it changed pretty much her entire personality while she was down there, especially regarding how "fate" functions and how arachne is affected by it in her capacity as master/servant of fate

i'm honestly very excited to see how this chapter ends i need to know what happens and especially concerning nefraea, because if she was initially a part of arachne as is stated, that makes her somewhat similar to the "puppets" (memories, romance, kindness) so it also implies that at any point in time, arachne could have taken back the power from nefraea at the cost of her existence, but chose not to because that would mean being "alone" again, without another person to talk/banter with, since even with "rea", the spider maid/servant that accompanies arachne and is tasked by her to remind her of what she must do, arachne really has nobody, and nefraea was her companion/counterpart that just. is there for her and i think about that a lot
thread_title: Arachne story rambles (arc2 ch5 spoilers for basically the ENTIRE THING)

content:
> “Few can find the threshold, fewer can cross it and live. But once you join the residents beyond the veil, you will be unbound. You will be free."
this is literally the introductory quote for Ultra, and to me, Arachne/Nefraea are both fighting against "fate", but whilst Arachne herself has given up after the destruction of her home world, Nefraea is the part of her that is still dreaming to be free/trying to free herself and I think that just says a lot that how despite being in multiple positions where both of them could easily have ended the other, they haven't, because they need each other.

going back to arachne's role as the master of fate/spinner of fate though, even back in the events at the end of ch4, where Arachne appears to Keeper whilst in Murphy's dream, she doesn't say anything, nor does she appear to properly be able to help keeper at all, aside from acting as just a symbolic figure/there as a witness, not speaking or doing anything other than watching keeper in those moments of limbo

it's like she said in the pv- the master of fate is also its servant, and she's trapped in that cycle of knowing everything that happens, having to orchestrate it, and also just as a permanent spectator trying to watch it all GOSH i love her so much

but there's also the whole lightbearer thing, with nefraea starting the church in this world, in order to awaken tohuwabohu, whom the believes will help free arachne because once the great one wakes up, arachne will essentially be "freed" since nothing will exist afterwards, but nefraea doesn't really see all that, just the "freedom" the awakening represents for the two of them as both a singular entity "arachne" and as individuals, now that arachne made the choice to split herself

rea as well, as the leng spider maid accompanying arachne throughout, tasked by arachne *herself* to make sure she stays on track, which might be the reason that nefraea exploded her in particular vs most of the other unnamed npcs-
thread_title: Arachne story rambles (arc2 ch5 spoilers for basically the ENTIRE THING)

content: she was the reason why arachne kept going, because she had been tasked to remind arachne to "stay on the right track", as it were, so nefraea might have held resentment towards that role she held in arachne's life, just as she did toward "memories", "kindness", and "romance", having mocked the latter three during the "play" she held in arachne's theater during 5-11
